fixes: Fixed invalid glob negation syntax ('!build/**/*.map') in artifacts:paths. GitLab CI does not support negation in paths; replaced with proper artifacts:exclude keyword.
Fixed deprecated Kustomize 'bases' keyword to 'resources' in overlay kustomization.yaml example.
Updated deprecated 'docker-compose' command (v1) to 'docker compose' (v2 plugin) in integration test example.

Fixed deprecated MySQL '--default-authentication-plugin=mysql_native_password' flag to '--authentication-policy=mysql_native_password'. The default_authentication_plugin option was deprecated in MySQL 8.0.27 and removed in 8.4.
Updated deprecated 'docker-compose' command (v1) to 'docker compose' (v2 plugin) throughout the post.
Updated 'apk add --no-cache docker-compose' to 'apk add --no-cache docker-cli-compose' for Alpine package name.

Fixed 'Dynamic Parallel Count' section: 'parallel: $PARALLEL_COUNT' is not valid because the parallel keyword requires a static integer and does not support CI/CD variable expansion. Replaced with a parent-child pipeline approach that generates the child pipeline YAML dynamically.
Fixed malformed code fence in release description YAML block - closing fence had '```bash' instead of '```', and trailing '```text' was changed to '```'.

Updated deprecated 'runnerRegistrationToken' Helm chart value to 'runnerToken' with runner authentication token (runner registration tokens are deprecated since GitLab 16.0, disabled by default in 17.0).
Updated deprecated '--registration-token' CLI flag to '--token' with glrt- prefixed authentication token.
Fixed invalid 'tag_list' field in config.toml example - tags are managed via GitLab UI/API or during registration, not in config.toml. Replaced with registration command example.

2025-12-22-spring-boot-transaction-manag.notes: Fixed Pitfall 4: Changed 'javax.transaction.Transactional' to 'jakarta.transaction.Transactional' to match Spring Boot 3.x/Jakarta EE namespace.

2025-12-22-unit-testing-mockito-spring-b.notes: @MockBean is deprecated since Spring Boot 3.4 in favor of @MockitoBean. Code still compiles and works but is outdated practice.

Replaced deprecated AddFluentValidationAutoValidation() with manual validation pattern
Corrected GetValueOrDefault version from 'C# 7.1+' to '.NET Core 2.0+ / .NET Standard 2.1+'
Added version annotations for ThrowIfNullOrEmpty (.NET 7+) and ThrowIfLessThanOrEqual (.NET 8+)
Added deprecation note for Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Polly and AddTransientHttpErrorPolicy in favor of Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Resilience

issues_found: FIXED: Bash inline comments after backslash line continuations in docker network create and docker service create commands. Bash does not support `\ # comment` syntax - the backslash becomes part of the comment and breaks line continuation. Comments moved to preceding lines.
FIXED: Bash inline comments after backslash line continuations in docker run -v commands. Comments moved to preceding lines.

FIXED: `docker builder prune --keep-storage 10GB` and `--keep-storage 10000` use the deprecated --keep-storage flag. Updated to --reserved-space (the current flag as of Docker buildx 2025). Also corrected the unitless numeric form to use a valid size string.
FIXED: Bash inline comments after backslash line continuations in docker run and docker run syslog driver commands. Comments moved to preceding lines.
FIXED: Bash inline comments after backslash line continuations in docker buildx build commands (basic multi-platform, multi-tag, and cache-from/cache-to examples). Comments moved to preceding lines.

FIXED: Two JSON blocks embedded inside bash code blocks contained JSON comments (# comment syntax), which is invalid JSON. The pasta/daemon.json block had `"containerd-snapshotter": true  # Enable modern features` and the storage config block had `"data-root": "/data/docker-rootless"  # Custom data directory`. Both were separated into proper bash + json code blocks.

FIXED: The `deploy` section (with `update_config`, `replicas`, `order: start-first`, `failure_action: rollback`) is part of the Compose Deploy Specification and is only honored by Docker Swarm via `docker stack deploy`. When using `docker compose up` in standalone mode this section is ignored. Added a prominent note clarifying this requirement and updated the complete production example to remove inline YAML comments that could confuse readers about the Swarm requirement.
FIXED: Bash inline comments after backslash line continuations in multiple docker run commands (backup, restore, restic restore, borg backup/restore). Comments moved to preceding lines.

Updated velero-plugin-for-gcp version from v1.8.0 to v1.11.0
Updated velero-plugin-for-microsoft-azure version from v1.8.0 to v1.11.0

CSI volume backup section: removed reference to archived velero-plugin-for-csi:v0.6.0 separate plugin; noted that as of Velero v1.14 the CSI plugin is built-in and no separate plugin is needed; --features=EnableCSI flag retained as it is still required for CSI integration
Added inline comment to cluster-autoscaler image tag (v1.28.0) clarifying that the minor version must match the Kubernetes cluster version
Replaced deprecated bitnami/etcd:3.5 image with registry.k8s.io/etcd:3.5.21-0 in the CronJob spec (bitnami/etcd is deprecated and moved to bitnamilegacy/etcd)
Updated etcdctl download URL in S3 backup CronJob from outdated v3.5.9 to v3.5.21

Updated ImageRepository, ImagePolicy, and ImageUpdateAutomation apiVersions from image.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1beta2 to image.toolkit.fluxcd.io/v1 — the v1beta2 API was removed in Flux v2.8 and the stable v1 API is now required
Updated NVIDIA device plugin image from nvcr.io/nvidia/k8s-device-plugin:v0.14.3 to v0.17.1 (current stable release as of 2025)

Corrected Kubernetes version requirements: changed 'Kubernetes 1.18+ (ephemeral containers in beta)' to 'Kubernetes 1.23+ (ephemeral containers in beta, requires EphemeralContainers feature gate)' for accuracy — ephemeral containers were not beta-available in 1.18, beta support requires 1.23
vault write auth/kubernetes/config included 'issuer' parameter which is deprecated since Vault 1.9.0 and no longer required. Fixed by removing the issuer line and adding a deprecation note.
security.istio.io/v1beta1 used for PeerAuthentication (2 instances) - updated to security.istio.io/v1 (stable since Istio 1.22)
security.istio.io/v1beta1 used for AuthorizationPolicy - updated to security.istio.io/v1
Deprecated istioctl authn tls-check command (removed in Istio 1.6+) - replaced with istioctl x describe
Observability addon URLs referenced release-1.20 - updated to release-1.24

issues_found.description: AES-256-GCM IV size was set to 16 bytes via crypto.randomBytes(16). NIST SP 800-38D recommends 12 bytes (96 bits) for GCM mode for maximum efficiency and interoperability. Using 16-byte IVs causes them to be hashed down to 12 bytes internally anyway and creates interoperability issues with some implementations.
issues_found.fix: Changed crypto.randomBytes(16) to crypto.randomBytes(12) with explanatory comment citing NIST SP 800-38D

issues_found.description: Used non-existent `trace.active()` method to set parent context. OpenTelemetry JS API has no `trace.active()`. The correct method is `context.active()` from the `@opentelemetry/api` context module. Also, `startSpan` does not accept a `parent` option; the parent context is the third argument.
issues_found.fix_applied: Changed `const { trace, SpanStatusCode }` import to `const { trace, context, SpanStatusCode }`. Replaced `parent: trace.setSpan(trace.active(), span)` option with `const parentCtx = trace.setSpan(context.active(), span)` and passed it as the third argument to `startSpan`.

issues_found.description: Video processing section used `const Queue = require('bull')`. The bull package is in maintenance mode (no new features). The post already uses BullMQ (bullmq) everywhere else. Fixed to use `const { Queue } = require('bullmq')` with proper BullMQ connection config.

File type detection used `const fileType = require('file-type')` and `fileType.fromBuffer(buffer)`. Since file-type v19+, the package is ESM-only and cannot be loaded with CommonJS require(). Fixed to use dynamic `import('file-type')` inside the async handler and updated API call to `fileTypeFromBuffer(buffer)` (renamed from `fromBuffer` in v17).

BullMQ shutdown example called `worker.getJobs(['active'])`. getJobs() is a method on Queue, not on Worker. Calling it on a Worker instance throws a TypeError at runtime. Fixed by adding a Queue instance for the same queue and calling getJobs() on that instead.

Three Dockerfile stages used `npm ci --only=production` which is deprecated since npm v8.3.0 and produces a WARN in npm v9+. The correct flag is `--omit=dev`. All three occurrences (NestJS prod-deps stage, TypeScript prod-deps stage, and layer caching example) were fixed to `--omit=dev`. Note: the first occurrence in the multi-stage pattern section already correctly used `--omit=dev`.

issues_found: kubeProxyReplacement: true (bare boolean YAML) was invalid for Cilium 1.15 Helm chart which expects a string. Fixed to kubeProxyReplacement: "true" in values file and --set kubeProxyReplacement="true" in CLI command.
Used 'go get' to install bpf2go CLI tool; fixed to 'go install' per Go 1.16+ best practice

issues_found.description: Post states 'Root privileges or CAP_BPF capability' as a prerequisite while also listing kernel 4.14 as minimum. CAP_BPF was introduced in Linux kernel 5.8 (to separate BPF functionality from CAP_SYS_ADMIN). On kernels 4.14 to 5.7, users need CAP_SYS_ADMIN or root, not CAP_BPF which did not yet exist. The claim was technically misleading for users on pre-5.8 kernels.
issues_found.fix: Added clarifying note: 'CAP_BPF was introduced in kernel 5.8; on older kernels, CAP_SYS_ADMIN or root is required'

issues_found: PodMonitor.queue field type was workqueue.RateLimitingInterface but initialized with workqueue.NewRateLimitingQueue(DefaultTypedControllerRateLimiter[string]()); fixed field type to workqueue.TypedRateLimitingInterface[string] and constructor to workqueue.NewTypedRateLimitingQueue
processNext used obj.(string) type assertion with untyped queue; fixed to use key directly from typed queue Get()
COSIGN_EXPERIMENTAL=1 prefix on cosign sign-blob command is deprecated since cosign 2.0 (keyless signing is now stable by default); replaced with explanatory comment

issues_found.description: `grpc.DialContext` is deprecated since gRPC-Go v1.64 in favor of `grpc.NewClient`. The deprecated function also accepted a context as first argument and `grpc.WithBlock()` which is discouraged.
issues_found.fix_applied: Replaced `grpc.DialContext(ctx, addr, ...)` with `grpc.NewClient(addr, ...)` and removed the deprecated `grpc.WithBlock()` option.

issues_found.description: Used outdated `go.opentelemetry.io/otel/semconv/v1.21.0` package instead of the current `v1.24.0` used consistently across other Go posts in this set.

issues_found.location: gRPC client connection setup in main function
issues_found.description: `grpc.Dial` is deprecated since gRPC-Go v1.64 in favor of `grpc.NewClient`. The deprecated `grpc.Dial` establishes the connection eagerly and has different behavior than `grpc.NewClient` which creates connections lazily.

issues_found: ansible.cfg used deprecated 'callback_whitelist' setting - replaced with 'callbacks_enabled' (callback_whitelist was renamed in Ansible 2.10 and removed in ansible-core 2.15)

issues_found.fix: Replaced both grpc.Dial calls with grpc.NewClient.

issues_found.description: Used deprecated grpc.Dial at line ~321. Deprecated since grpc-go v1.63.

Used deprecated grpc.DialContext in three locations: pool initialization loop (line ~120), checkAndReconnect function (line ~193), and service config example (line ~1179). Also used deprecated grpc.Dial in anti-patterns example (line ~1127). Deprecated since grpc-go v1.63.
issues_found.fix: Replaced all grpc.DialContext calls with grpc.NewClient (removed context parameter as NewClient connects lazily). Replaced grpc.Dial with grpc.NewClient in anti-patterns example.

issues_found.description: Used deprecated grpc.DialContext in run() function (line ~773) with grpc.WithBlock() option. Missing 'google.golang.org/protobuf/types/known/durationpb' import in error helpers code block that uses durationpb.Duration in RateLimitError function.
issues_found.fix: Replaced grpc.DialContext with grpc.NewClient (removed context parameter and grpc.WithBlock() option). Added durationpb import to the error helpers code block.

issues_found: Fixed: Used deprecated prometheus.NewProcessCollector and prometheus.NewGoCollector APIs (from prometheus package directly). Updated to use collectors.NewProcessCollector(collectors.ProcessCollectorOpts{}) and collectors.NewGoCollector() from the github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus/collectors package, and added the collectors import.

Claimed Kubernetes 1.25+ introduced nftables support in kube-proxy. Incorrect: nftables mode was introduced as alpha in Kubernetes 1.29 and graduated to beta in 1.31. Fixed version references throughout.
kubectl version --short is deprecated since Kubernetes 1.28. Fixed to kubectl version.
nat table postrouting/prerouting chains used numeric priorities -100/100. Updated to use keyword aliases dstnat/srcnat for clarity and correctness.

Coexistence config comment stated 'priority 10' is 'Higher priority than iptables' which is wrong: in nftables lower numbers = higher priority. Fixed priority to -10 so it runs before default iptables-nft (priority 0) and corrected the comment.

issues_found.description: JSX.Element return type is deprecated in React 18+; changed to React.ReactElement

issues_found: Production config example included 'PostUp = ip route add 10.200.0.0/16 dev wg0' for routes already covered by AllowedIPs. wg-quick automatically creates kernel routes for all AllowedIPs, so duplicate PostUp route additions would fail with EEXIST. Fixed by replacing with a comment explaining the behavior and showing an example only for routes outside AllowedIPs.
nftables nat table used 'table inet nat' family which requires kernel 5.2+ for NAT/masquerade. Changed to 'table ip nat' for broader compatibility, matching the IPv4-only masquerade use case.

ApolloServerPlugin type import is correct for v4. startStandaloneServer is mentioned as an alternative but the post uses the production-recommended express middleware pattern.

Zustand store creation pattern create<T>((set) => ...) is incorrect for TypeScript; must use double-parenthesis pattern create<T>()((set) => ...) for proper type inference. Fixed in 5 occurrences.

All logrotate directives are valid: rotate, size, compress, missingok, notifempty, delaycompress, sharedscripts, postrotate/endscript, prerotate/endscript, lastaction/endscript, firstaction/endscript, copytruncate, dateext, minsize, maxsize, create, nocreate. Configuration file locations (/etc/logrotate.conf, /etc/logrotate.d/) are correct. State file at /var/lib/logrotate/status is accurate.

2026-01-15-configure-mdns-avahi-ubuntu.notes: No errors found. Avahi installation (avahi-daemon avahi-utils libnss-mdns) is correct. Configuration file at /etc/avahi/avahi-daemon.conf is accurate. avahi-browse command with -a, -r, -t flags is correct. avahi-resolve for hostname/IP resolution is accurate. Service XML file format in /etc/avahi/services/ is correct. mDNS multicast address (224.0.0.251:5353) and nsswitch.conf mdns4_minimal configuration are accurate.

2026-01-15-configure-multipath-io-ubuntu.notes: No errors found. multipath-tools and multipath-tools-boot package installation is correct. multipathd daemon management via systemctl is accurate. /etc/multipath.conf configuration structure (defaults, blacklist, blacklist_exceptions, devices, multipaths sections) is correct. multipath -ll, multipathd show paths, multipath -r commands are accurate. Blacklisting local devices is correctly emphasized. Path grouping policies (failover, multibus, group_by_prio) and path selectors (round-robin, queue-length, service-time) are all valid.

issues_found.description: Docker build stage used 'npm ci --only=production' which skips devDependencies needed for building; changed to plain 'npm ci'

All three patroni.yml node configurations used 'etcd:' (etcd API v2 key). etcd API v2 is disabled by default from etcd v3.4 onward and will be completely removed in etcd v3.6. Modern Ubuntu installations will have etcd 3.4+ which requires 'etcd3:' to use the v3 API.
issues_found.fix: Replaced all three occurrences of 'etcd:' with 'etcd3:' in the patroni.yml configurations for pg-node1, pg-node2, and pg-node3.

issues_found.description: useOfflineAPI hook used 'useState(() => {...}, [])' with a cleanup-returning callback and dependency array, which is invalid useState usage; this is a side-effect pattern that belongs in useEffect. Changed to useEffect and added useEffect to imports.

issues_found: daemon.json example included 'experimental: true' as required for ip6tables. This is only required for Docker versions prior to v27. Docker v27+ (released 2024) no longer requires the experimental flag. Added clarifying notes to both daemon.json examples.

issues_found.description: Used deprecated React 17 API 'unmountComponentAtNode' from 'react-dom' and 'render' from 'react-dom'; replaced with React 18 'createRoot' from 'react-dom/client' and 'root.unmount()'
Docker build stage used invalid npm flag 'npm ci --only=production=false'; changed to plain 'npm ci'

The `aws route53 create-key-signing-key` command was missing the required `--caller-reference` parameter in two places (initial KSK creation and KSK rotation step). The `--caller-reference` is a required parameter per AWS CLI documentation — it must be a unique string (1-128 chars) identifying the request to prevent duplicate key creation.
issues_found.fix_applied: Added `--caller-reference "ksk-example-com-2026-$(date +%s)"` and `--caller-reference "ksk-example-com-2027-$(date +%s)"` to both create-key-signing-key invocations. Also updated the summary reference table to include `--caller-reference UNIQUE_STRING`.

2026-01-15-install-apache-activemq-ubunt.notes: Install path /opt/activemq correct. Admin console URL at http://localhost:8161/admin correct (port 8161). Binary tarball installation steps accurate. No errors found.

2026-01-15-install-apache-apisix-ubuntu.notes: FIXED: Main config.yaml example used old APISIX 2.x format with top-level admin_key: and apisix.enable_admin. Updated to APISIX 3.x deployment: structure with admin_key under deployment.admin and etcd under deployment.etcd. Admin API endpoint port 9180 and /apisix/admin prefix are correct.

2026-01-15-install-apache-pulsar-ubuntu.notes: Binary tarball installation correct. pulsar standalone command correct. Java 17 requirement accurate for Pulsar 3.x. Admin API at port 8080 correct. No errors found.

2026-01-15-install-configure-apache-ubun.notes: a2ensite, a2enmod, apache2ctl configtest all correct. Virtual host configuration valid. SSLProtocol directives correct. No errors found.

issues_found.description: The `aws lambda invoke --payload '{"key": "value"}'` command was missing the `--cli-binary-format raw-in-base64-out` flag. In AWS CLI v2, all binary/blob parameters including `--payload` default to base64 encoding. Passing raw inline JSON without this flag causes an 'Invalid base64' error at runtime.
issues_found.fix_applied: Added `--cli-binary-format raw-in-base64-out` flag and an explanatory comment to the lambda invoke command example.

issues_found.description: schema_config used deprecated 'store: boltdb-shipper' and 'schema: v11'. Current recommendation is 'store: tsdb' and 'schema: v13' (required by Loki 3.x for structured metadata).
issues_found.location: Basic Loki Configuration and Production Configuration schema_config blocks

issues_found: vault kv destroy -versions=all secret/myapp is incorrect syntax. '-versions=all' is not a valid flag value; vault kv destroy requires specific version numbers. The correct command to remove all versions and metadata is 'vault kv metadata delete secret/myapp'. Fixed.
'vault operator seal-status' is not a valid Vault CLI command. The correct command to check seal/unseal status is 'vault status'. Fixed.

issues_found.description: Downloads Kafka 3.6.1 specifically. Readers should check https://kafka.apache.org/downloads for the latest release. Configuration and commands are correct for the referenced version.
kafka-metadata.sh command shown for checking broker health. This is an internal tool; kafka-broker-api-versions.sh is more appropriate for cluster health checks and is also shown in the guide.

2026-01-15-install-configure-openldap-ub.notes: Uses modern slapd.d (cn=config) format correctly throughout. ldapmodify -Y EXTERNAL -H ldapi:/// for config changes is correct. LDIF format valid. slapcat backup commands correct. No errors found.

issues_found: Fixed: Multiple incorrect uses of histogram_quantile() with probe_duration_seconds_bucket. The blackbox exporter's probe_duration_seconds is a Gauge (not a Histogram), so it does not expose _bucket, _count, or _sum series. Replaced all histogram_quantile(0.x, rate(probe_duration_seconds_bucket[5m])) expressions with correct avg_over_time/max_over_time/quantile approaches. Fixed in recording rules (3 rules), PromQL query section (2 queries), and Grafana heatmap panel JSON.

fixes: Added deprecation notice: Flipper was deprecated in React Native 0.73 and removed from new app templates in 0.74. Updated prerequisites section and configuration intro to reflect supported version range (RN 0.62-0.72).

2026-01-15-react-native-android-bridge.notes: Correctly uses @ReactMethod annotation and ReactApplicationContext throughout.

2026-01-15-react-native-asyncstorage-mmk.notes: Correctly uses @react-native-async-storage/async-storage (not deprecated @react-native-community/async-storage). MMKV API (set, getString, getNumber, getBoolean, delete, contains, getAllKeys, clearAll) matches react-native-mmkv package API.

2026-01-15-react-native-auth-flows-navig.notes: Correctly uses React Navigation v6 API: NavigationContainer, createNativeStackNavigator, useNavigation.

2026-01-15-react-native-background-tasks.notes: Correctly uses react-native-background-fetch (not deprecated react-native-background-job). AsyncStorage uses correct package.

2026-01-15-react-native-beta-testing.notes: Correctly uses keytool -genkeypair. Firebase Crashlytics uses @react-native-firebase/crashlytics.

2026-01-15-react-native-biometric-auth.notes: Correctly uses react-native-biometrics package (not a scoped @react-native-biometrics package). expo-local-authentication API is correct.

2026-01-15-react-native-bottom-sheet.notes: BottomSheetModal from @gorhom/bottom-sheet is valid in both v4 and v5 APIs. BottomSheetModalProvider usage is correct.

2026-01-15-react-native-bundle-size.notes: Metro bundle command uses correct --entry-file and --bundle-output flags. react-native-bundle-visualizer usage is correct.

issues_found: actions/checkout@v4 updated to actions/checkout@v6 (all occurrences)
actions/setup-node@v4 updated to actions/setup-node@v6 (all occurrences)
actions/setup-java@v4 updated to actions/setup-java@v5
actions/cache@v4 updated to actions/cache@v5 (all occurrences)
codecov/codecov-action@v4 updated to codecov/codecov-action@v5

2026-01-15-setup-email-notifications-pos.notes: Postfix relayhost = [smtp.gmail.com]:587 format correct. sasl_passwd file and postmap usage correct. smtp_sasl_security_options and TLS settings valid. No errors found.

2026-01-15-setup-mail-server-postfix-dov.notes: Dovecot protocols = imap lmtp sieve is correct modern configuration (lmtp replaces older deliver). listen = *, :: correct. Postfix TLS protocol exclusions valid. smtpd_sasl_type = dovecot correct. No errors found.

issues_found: 'sudo ip link set br0 type bridge priority 100' is incorrect syntax for setting bridge STP priority. The 'ip link set ... type bridge priority' form sets bridge port (slave) priority, not the bridge device priority. Fixed to use 'brctl setbridgeprio br0 100' with an alternative sysfs method.
High Availability Queues section used ha-mode policy which was deprecated in RabbitMQ 3.9 and removed in 4.0. Added deprecation note and quorum queue alternative.

2026-01-15-setup-tftp-server-ubuntu.notes: tftpd-hpa configuration in /etc/default/tftpd-hpa with correct TFTP_USERNAME, TFTP_DIRECTORY, TFTP_ADDRESS, TFTP_OPTIONS format. systemd service management correct. in.tftpd path for xinetd mode is accurate. No errors found.

issues_found.description: Docker image names used the Docker Hub short form 'elasticsearch:8.11.0' and 'kibana:8.11.0'. For Elasticsearch 8.x, the official images must be referenced from the Elastic Docker registry. All occurrences were updated to 'docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.11.0' and 'docker.elastic.co/kibana/kibana:8.11.0'.

Removed 'version:' field from all five Docker Compose snippets (Basic Setup, KRaft Mode, Multi-Broker Cluster, With Kafka UI, Complete Production Setup). The version field is obsolete in Docker Compose v2 and generates a warning.

Grafana Tempo datasource URL used wrong port 3100. Tempo's default HTTP port is 3200 (changed from 3100 to avoid collision with Loki's default port). This affected two places: the datasource provisioning URL and the querier external_endpoints configuration.

Bitnami values file uses 'kraft.enabled: false' and 'zookeeper.enabled: true' pattern. In current Bitnami kafka chart (v30+), KRaft is enabled by default. When authoring new deployments the kraft section should be reviewed against the chart version in use.

Workflow template referenced the deprecated/archived 'stable' Helm repository (helm repo add stable https://charts.helm.sh/stable). The stable chart repository was deprecated in 2020 and is now archived/read-only.
issues_found.fix: Removed the deprecated 'helm repo add stable https://charts.helm.sh/stable' line from the workflow template. The bitnami repo add is sufficient.

issues_found.description: Used deprecated cert-manager Helm value 'installCRDs: true'. Since cert-manager v1.15.0, installCRDs is deprecated in favor of 'crds.enabled: true' (with optional 'crds.keep: true').
issues_found.fix: Updated install command from '--set installCRDs=true' to '--set crds.enabled=true'. Updated production values.yaml to use 'crds: { enabled: true, keep: true }' block instead of 'installCRDs: true'.

issues_found.description: The first code example used '--keyring ~/.gnupg/secring.gpg' without noting that GPG 2.1+ does not create secring.gpg by default. Users would get 'private key not found' errors without first exporting: 'gpg --export-secret-keys > ~/.gnupg/secring.gpg'.
issues_found.fix: Added a comment before the secring.gpg example explaining that GPG 2.1+ requires an export step first: 'gpg --export-secret-keys > ~/.gnupg/secring.gpg'

issues_found: actions/checkout@v4 updated to actions/checkout@v6 (all occurrences)
github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3 updated to github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v4 (2 occurrences - v3 deprecated in favor of v4)

issues_found.description: Production Bitnami values use 'kraft.enabled: true' with separate controller and broker replicaCounts, which is accurate for current Bitnami kafka chart v28+.
issues_found.location: Option 1: Bitnami Kafka Chart - Production Values section

issues_found: ExternalSecret remoteRef.key values used 'secret/data/app/database' and 'secret/data/app/api' incorrectly. When the SecretStore specifies path: 'secret' and version: 'v2', the remoteRef.key should be just the relative path without the mount name or /data/ prefix (e.g., 'app/database'). ESO handles the KV v2 /data/ path translation internally. Fixed to 'app/database' and 'app/api'.
ExternalSecret dataFrom.extract.key and dataFrom.find.path similarly used 'secret/data/app/config' and 'secret/data/app/' respectively. Fixed to 'app/config' and 'app/'.

issues_found.description: grafana/loki-stack Helm chart is deprecated. The current recommended chart is grafana/loki. Added deprecation note in comment.

issues_found: Fixed: configs.params.server.insecure=true was missing dot-escaping in --set flag; corrected to configs.params."server\.insecure"=true

ExternalSecret remoteRef.key values used 'secret/data/myapp/config' incorrectly. When the ClusterSecretStore specifies path: 'secret' and version: 'v2', the remoteRef.key should be just the relative path 'myapp/config' without the mount name or /data/ prefix. ESO handles the KV v2 /data/ path translation internally. Fixed.
ExternalSecret dataFrom.extract.key used 'secret/data/myapp/config' incorrectly. Fixed to 'myapp/config'.

issues_found.description: Dashboard provisioning provider had folder: 'Provisioned' and folderUid: 'provisioned' set alongside foldersFromFilesStructure: true. Per Grafana docs, when foldersFromFilesStructure is enabled, folder and folderUid must be empty strings or unset; otherwise Grafana will fail with a validation error.

issues_found: win_chocolatey module was incorrectly referenced as 'ansible.windows.win_chocolatey' - the correct FQCN is 'chocolatey.chocolatey.win_chocolatey' from the separate 'chocolatey.chocolatey' collection (win_chocolatey is NOT in the ansible.windows collection). Fixed all occurrences and added 'ansible-galaxy collection install chocolatey.chocolatey' to prerequisites.

community.windows.win_firewall is deprecated and transitioning to ansible.windows.win_firewall - updated the roles/windows_base example to use 'ansible.windows.win_firewall' instead of 'community.windows.win_firewall'

issues_found.description: MirrorMaker 2 is started with bin/connect-mirror-maker.sh, which is correct. The mm2.properties format and options (sync.group.offsets, emit.checkpoints, etc.) are accurate for Kafka 2.7+ MirrorMaker 2.

ECK operator version 2.11.1 referenced in kubectl install URLs is from the 2.x series. The current latest ECK operator is 3.x. The version used in the post is a valid historical 2.x release and does not affect correctness of the documented patterns.

The 'moving_avg' pipeline aggregation was deprecated in Elasticsearch 6.4 and removed in Elasticsearch 8.0. The post used moving_avg in the 'Moving Average' section. This was replaced with the correct 'moving_fn' aggregation using 'MovingFunctions.unweightedAvg(values)' as the script, along with a note explaining the change.

The ILM policy for audit logs used the 'freeze' action in the 'cold' phase. The freeze/unfreeze API and the freeze ILM action were removed in Elasticsearch 8.0. The action was replaced with 'readonly' which is the appropriate cold-phase action for Elasticsearch 8.x.

The 'Set Up Monitoring' section used 'xpack.monitoring.collection.enabled: true' as a cluster setting, which was deprecated in Elasticsearch 7.16 and is deprecated in 8.x. The recommended approach is to use Elastic Agent or Metricbeat for monitoring. The section was updated to remove the deprecated call and replace it with current cluster stats API commands.
grafana/loki-stack Helm chart is deprecated. The current recommended chart is grafana/loki. Added deprecation note in comment.

kafka-metadata.sh is used for checking cluster membership. This is a low-level internal tool. For production use, kafka-broker-api-versions.sh or the Admin API is more appropriate. The guide also shows the correct alternative.

authorizer.class.name=kafka.security.authorizer.AclAuthorizer is correct for ZooKeeper-based clusters. For KRaft-mode clusters, org.apache.kafka.metadata.authorizer.StandardAuthorizer should be used instead. The post does not explicitly call this out.

Removed deprecated imports from the Python implementation code block: 'from confluent_kafka.avro import AvroProducer, AvroConsumer' and 'from confluent_kafka.avro.cached_schema_registry_client import CachedSchemaRegistryClient'. These classes are deprecated since confluent-kafka-python v2.0.2 and the code was already using the correct modern SerializingProducer/DeserializingConsumer API below them.
Removed deprecated 'version: 3' field from Docker Compose setup. Obsolete in Docker Compose v2.
issues_found.location: Setting Up Schema Registry - Docker Compose Setup section

issues_found.description: Removed deprecated 'version: 3.8' field from Docker Compose. Obsolete in Docker Compose v2.
issues_found.location: Setting Up Kafka with Docker section

issues_found.description: Added missing KAFKA_LISTENERS: PLAINTEXT://0.0.0.0:9092 environment variable to cp-kafka container. Without KAFKA_LISTENERS, the broker may bind to the wrong interface. KAFKA_ADVERTISED_LISTENERS alone is insufficient for proper broker configuration with the Confluent image.
issues_found.location: Setting Up Kafka with Docker - docker-compose.yml

issues_found.description: Duplicate 'secureJsonData' key in Grafana datasource YAML configuration. YAML does not allow duplicate keys; the second definition would silently override the first, causing basicAuthPassword to be lost. Merged both secureJsonData blocks into one.
issues_found.location: Grafana Data Source with Authentication section (grafana-provisioning/datasources/loki.yaml example)

issues_found: 'molecule init role my_role --driver-name docker' command was removed in Molecule 6.0. The correct approach is to first use 'ansible-galaxy role init my_role' then run 'molecule init scenario -d docker' inside the role directory. Updated the initialization section to reflect the current workflow.

Prerequisites incorrectly listed 'pip install docker docker-compose'. The 'docker-compose' pip package installs the deprecated Docker Compose v1 (EOL since July 2022) and is broken on many systems. The docker_compose_v2 module only needs the Docker CLI with the Compose plugin. Fixed to 'pip install docker' with a clarifying comment.
docker-compose.yml.j2 template included obsolete top-level 'version: 3.8' field. Docker Compose V2 (v2.25.0+) reports this as obsolete - removed the version line and added explanatory comment.

AWX Operator installation used the 'devel' branch URL ('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ansible/awx-operator/devel/deploy/awx-operator.yaml') which is not recommended for production use. Official AWX documentation recommends using a specific release tag via kustomize. Updated to use the kustomize-based installation approach with a specific version tag (2.19.1), which is the recommended production installation method.

fixes: Added missing 'sync/atomic' import to counter benchmark that used atomic.AddInt64

issues_found.description: asyncio.get_event_loop() used inside async main() function - deprecated in Python 3.10+
issues_found.location: Line 464, 'Running Blocking Code in Async Context' section
issues_found.fix_applied: Replaced asyncio.get_event_loop() with asyncio.get_running_loop() inside the async main() function

issues_found: x-queue-mode=lazy is ignored in RabbitMQ 3.12+. Added a prominent deprecation notice at the top of the post explaining that in 3.12+ all classic queues behave like lazy queues and this setting has no effect.

Troubleshooting section used 'rabbitmqctl quorum_status <queue-name>' and 'rabbitmqctl delete_member/add_member' — the correct tool is 'rabbitmq-queues', not 'rabbitmqctl'. Fixed to use 'rabbitmq-queues quorum_status <queue-name>', 'rabbitmq-queues delete_member', and 'rabbitmq-queues add_member'.

rabbitmq.conf configuration blocks were labeled as 'erlang' code blocks with '%%' Erlang comment style. Fixed to use 'ini' label with '#' comment style which is correct for the sysctl-format rabbitmq.conf.

rabbitmq.conf configuration block was labeled as 'erlang' with '%%' Erlang comment syntax. The rabbitmq.conf file uses sysctl format with '#' comments. Fixed the code block label to 'ini' and converted all '%%' comments to '#' comments.
Multiple rabbitmq.conf configuration blocks were labeled as 'erlang' with '%%' Erlang comment syntax instead of the correct 'ini' label and '#' comment style. Fixed all affected blocks.
Two rabbitmq.conf configuration blocks were labeled as 'erlang' with '%%' Erlang comment syntax instead of the correct 'ini' label and '#' comment style. Fixed both affected blocks.

ExternalSecret remoteRef.key values used 'secret/data/database' incorrectly. When a SecretStore specifies path: 'secret' and version: 'v2', the remoteRef.key should be just 'database' without the mount name or /data/ prefix. Fixed.
Fixed: Added clarification that the separate v0.4.1 install URL is for legacy pre-2.3 ArgoCD only; the standalone applicationset repository is archived and ApplicationSets are bundled in ArgoCD 2.3+

Fixed: 'uses: infracost/actions/comment@v1' does not exist as a GitHub Actions sub-action. Replaced with correct CLI invocation 'infracost comment github' using the infracost CLI, which is the documented approach from infracost/actions.
Fixed: AppProject sourceRepos contained 'https://charts.helm.sh/stable' which is deprecated and no longer receives updates; replaced with 'https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami'

EventBuffer.initialize() called listCollections() on a collection object instead of the db object - fixed by storing this.db reference in constructor and using this.db.listCollections() and this.db.createCollection()
maxWaitQueueSize is not a valid option in the MongoDB Node.js driver (it exists in the C# driver but not Node.js) - removed from MongoClient options example

encryptionCipherMode and encryptionKeyFile were incorrectly placed under storage.wiredTiger.engineConfig - these belong under the security: section (MongoDB Enterprise). Also missing enableEncryption: true field. All three config blocks fixed to use correct security: section placement.
Fixed: clusterLabels field in Secret stringData is not a valid ArgoCD cluster secret field; moved cluster labels to metadata.labels where the ApplicationSet cluster generator reads them from
Fixed: Removed fictional argocd-agent Deployment manifest with incorrect image and flags; replaced with accurate description of the argocd-agent hub-and-spoke architecture and link to official docs

Fixed: Removed non-existent argocd.argoproj.io/parent-project annotation and Hierarchical Projects section; ArgoCD does not support project hierarchies via this annotation
Fixed: Removed fabricated audit.enabled ConfigMap field; ArgoCD does not expose this flag - audit logging is handled via Kubernetes API server audit logs

issues_found: Infrastructure Provisioning Workflow section incorrectly used community.general.ufw (Ubuntu/Debian firewall module) for RHEL hosts. RHEL uses firewalld, not UFW. Fixed by replacing both ufw tasks with ansible.posix.firewalld using port/tcp loop and a firewall-cmd --reload command.

Infrastructure Provisioning Workflow section used ansible.builtin.timezone which does not exist as a builtin module. The correct fully-qualified module name is community.general.timezone. Fixed accordingly.

The dracut add_drivers line listed 'vfio-pci' with a hyphen, but the correct Linux kernel module name is 'vfio_pci' (underscore). This matters because dracut/modprobe uses the underscore form; the hyphen form would fail to find or load the module. Fixed to 'vfio vfio_pci vfio_iommu_type1'.

results.notes: ceph-monstore-tool store-copy syntax verified correct. CRUSH map export/import commands accurate. OSD map backup via osdmaptool verified.
BlueStore cache ratio configuration verified. bluestore_cache_kv_ratio + bluestore_cache_meta_ratio sum is valid (data is implicit remainder). Settings are accurate for Reef/Squid.
CRUSH hierarchy commands, device class configuration, and custom rule syntax all verified correct.
ceph dashboard set-grafana-api-url is still valid (not deprecated). SSL configuration, RBAC setup, and module enable commands verified.
dm-crypt/LUKS OSD encryption workflow, HashiCorp Vault KMS integration, RBD encryption, and CephFS fscrypt configuration verified.
Verified via web search: ISA-L replacing Jerasure as default plugin in the Tentacle release is accurate. Jerasure, LRC, SHEC, Clay plugin configurations verified correct.

issues.description: bluestore_cache_data_ratio is not a valid Ceph configuration parameter
issues.fix: Removed bluestore_cache_data_ratio = 0.3 and replaced with comment explaining that data cache is implicitly 1.0 - bluestore_cache_kv_ratio. There is no such config key in Ceph BlueStore.

issues.description: rbd-mirror peer user creation used wrong mon capability profile
issues.location: Section: Creating Mirror User on Secondary Cluster, ceph auth get-or-create command
issues.fix: Changed mon 'profile rbd-mirror' to mon 'profile rbd-mirror-peer'. The rbd-mirror-peer profile is the correct capability for the peer authentication user created on the secondary cluster.
issues.reference: Ceph RBD mirroring docs: the rbd-mirror daemon itself uses profile rbd-mirror, while users created for cross-cluster peer authentication use profile rbd-mirror-peer

results.notes: RGW realm/zonegroup/zone hierarchy commands verified. radosgw-admin realm create, zonegroup create, zone create, period update --commit workflow is accurate.
Public/cluster network CIDR configuration, Messenger v2 encryption settings, firewall rules for ports 3300/6789/6800-7300 verified correct.
NVMe queue depth, bluestore_min_alloc_size_ssd = 4096, osd_op_threads tuning, and io_uring configuration verified appropriate for NVMe workloads.
Ceph auth commands for Glance, Cinder, and Nova use correct 'profile rbd' syntax. ceph.conf sections for OpenStack services verified. librbd configuration accurate.
OSD failure diagnosis workflow, ceph-volume lvm commands, OSD mark out/in/down commands, and recovery weight adjustment verified correct.
Performance diagnostic commands (ceph osd perf, rados bench, rbd bench) and slow request analysis workflow verified.
PG autoscaler commands, pg_num/pgp_num configuration, and osd_pool_default_pg_autoscale_mode settings verified. pgp_num is redundant in Nautilus+ but not incorrect.

2026-01-15-install-configure-grafana-ubu.note: Specified post path did not exist. Validated closest equivalent: 2026-01-15-install-configure-grafana-loki-ubuntu (already validated by prior run). No additional errors found.

2026-01-21-grafana-alerts-pagerduty.note: Specified post path did not exist. Validated closest equivalent: 2026-01-21-loki-alerts-slack-pagerduty. No errors found. PagerDuty Events API v2 integration key field (service_key) and Alertmanager pagerduty_configs format are correct.

2026-01-21-grafana-annotations-events.note: Specified post path did not exist. Validated closest equivalent: 2026-01-28-grafana-annotations. No errors found. Annotation API endpoints, field names (dashboardUID, panelId, time, timeEnd, tags, text), and datasource annotation YAML format are correct.

2026-01-21-grafana-dashboard-json-model.note: Specified post path did not exist. Validated closest equivalent: 2026-01-30-grafana-dashboard-json-model. Fixed: schemaVersion described as 'currently 39' — updated to clarify that values in the 39-42 range are common in 2025 depending on Grafana version.

2026-01-21-grafana-dashboard-versioning-.note: Specified post path did not exist. Validated closest equivalent: 2026-01-26-dashboard-versions-grafana. No errors found. Provisioning YAML format (apiVersion: 1), dashboard API paths, and grafana.ini [dashboards] section are all correct.

2026-01-21-grafana-datasource-provisioni.note: Specified post path did not exist. No exact match found. Datasource provisioning format (apiVersion: 1) shown across multiple existing posts is correct per official Grafana documentation.

2026-01-21-grafana-multi-tenant-setup.note: Specified post path did not exist. Validated closest equivalent: 2026-02-09-grafana-mimir-multi-tenant. No errors found. Mimir multi-tenancy config (multitenancy_enabled: true, X-Scope-OrgID header, per-tenant overrides) is accurate.

2026-01-21-grafana-oncall-alerting.note: Specified post path did not exist. Validated closest equivalent: 2026-01-26-oncall-integration-grafana. Fixed: Added note that Grafana OnCall OSS entered maintenance mode in March 2025 and will be archived March 24, 2026; Grafana Cloud IRM is the recommended replacement.

2026-01-21-grafana-public-dashboards.note: Specified post path did not exist. Validated closest equivalent: 2025-12-17-grafana-public-dashboards-no-password. Fixed: Added note that Grafana 11.x (September 2024) renamed 'Public Dashboards' to 'Externally Shared Dashboards'; the publicDashboards feature toggle is only needed for Grafana 9.x/10.x — in Grafana 11+ the feature is always enabled.

2026-01-21-grafana-rbac-permissions.note: Specified post path did not exist. Validated closest equivalent: 2026-01-26-rbac-policies-grafana. Fixed: Clarified that Grafana OSS includes a subset of RBAC (basic fixed roles and some access-control APIs), while fully custom roles with arbitrary permission scopes require Enterprise or Cloud Advanced — not Enterprise-only as originally stated.

2026-01-21-grafana-recording-rules-prome.note: Specified post path did not exist. Validated closest equivalent: 2026-02-09-grafana-recording-rules-mimir. No errors found. Mimir ruler API rule group YAML format, API endpoint (/prometheus/config/v1/rules/{namespace}), and recording rule naming convention (level:metric:aggregation) are correct.

2026-01-21-grafana-slo-dashboards.note: Specified post path did not exist. Validated closest equivalent: 2026-01-26-slo-dashboards-grafana. No errors found. PromQL for availability SLI, error budget, and burn-rate calculations is correct. Post correctly recommends recording rules for expensive 30d window queries.

2026-01-21-grafana-teams-permissions.note: Specified post path did not exist. No grafana-teams-permissions post found. Team management APIs (/api/teams, /api/teams/{id}/members) shown in related posts are correct per Grafana documentation.

2026-01-21-grafana-tempo-tracing.note: Specified post path did not exist. Validated closest equivalent: 2026-01-26-grafana-tempo-tracing. No errors found. Tempo config (HTTP port 3200, OTLP gRPC 4317/HTTP 4318, metrics_generator remote_write, TraceQL syntax) is correct. Datasource provisioning fields tracesToLogsV2 and serviceMap.datasourceUid are correct.

2026-01-22-grafana-promql-queries.note: Specified post path did not exist. Validated closest equivalent: 2026-02-09-grafana-alerting-promql. No errors found. PromQL for alerting (CPU/memory/error-rate, anomaly detection with offset, predict_linear, SLO burn-rate) is syntactically correct. Alert rule format uses Grafana unified alerting (Grafana 9+) correctly.
